steps.title: Distribute the monomial
steps.content: Multiply $x^{-7}y^5z^4$ by each term inside the parentheses: $$(x^{-7}y^5z^4)(x^3z^6)+(x^{-7}y^5z^4)(y^7z^{-9}).$$

steps.title: Add exponents on matching bases
steps.content: For the first product, $$x^{-7}x^3y^5z^4z^6=x^{-4}y^5z^{10}.$$ For the second product, $$x^{-7}y^5y^7z^4z^{-9}=x^{-7}y^{12}z^{-5}.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The product is $$x^{-4}y^5z^{10}+x^{-7}y^{12}z^{-5}.$$ This matches Choice B — $x^{-4}y^{5}z^{10}+x^{-7}y^{12}z^{-5}$.

steps.title: Why the exponents combine this way
steps.content: When multiplying powers with the same base, add the exponents. That is why $x^{-7}x^3=x^{-4}$, $y^5y^7=y^{12}$, and $z^4z^{-9}=z^{-5}$.
